claim: co-improvement-adapt correctly structures its lifecycle and how it calls other skills/helpers: (1) memory writes go only through subagent($persist-rubrics-context) using a thin Request/Response client adapter and callee call-contract; (2) skill-creator init/quick_validate are only for local-skill materialization (State 1.5); (3) reflection is an internal procedure (references), not a separate skill call; (4) dependency is one-way (adapt imports persist; adapt does not own write engine).

criteria.text: Learn interrupt and State 3 explicitly spawn subagent($persist-rubrics-context) with Request builders and Response merge documented.

/Users/admin/.codex/skills/co-improvement-adapt/SKILL.md:128-131 State 3: write engine remains subagent($persist-rubrics-context); pending rows use caller_tag state3-pending-flush and spawn subagent; missed-candidate scan uses caller_tag state3-missed-scan.

/Users/admin/.codex/skills/co-improvement-adapt/references/persist-learnings-skill.md:12-13 pipeline: host event → build Request → subagent($persist-rubrics-context) → Response → merge session log → resume host.

criteria.reasoning: Both primary write paths (learn interrupt and State 3 flush/scan) explicitly name subagent($persist-rubrics-context), and the thin client adapter documents Request builders plus Response merge steps. Evidence is direct in SKILL.md and persist-learnings-skill.md.

Adapter Request JSON examples reuse callee field names as builders, not as a competing schema definition.
